checks.cmd: python3 -m py_compile scripts/workflow_validate.py scripts/workflow_validate_core.py scripts/workflow_checks.py scripts/workflow_checks_core.py scripts/workflow_detect.py scripts/workflow_utils.py scripts/workflow_render.py scripts/workflow_hooks.py scripts/workflow_memory.py
checks.name: lint

patternCompliance.note: All new code uses stdlib re/pathlib only, no external dependencies
_validate_skills() follows the same (root, findings, touched) signature and Finding dataclass as all other _validate_* functions
performance-review.md references sub-skills by path, never duplicates their content
All 3 PLANs have JSON contracts, all 3 SUMMARYs have JSON contracts

principleNotes.note: Parser handles edge cases (no frontmatter, malformed YAML, missing fields) gracefully with empty defaults. Validation integrates cleanly into existing pipeline. Schema rename matches runtime code.
No user input handling. Local file reads only. Regex patterns are compile-time constants. No secrets, no injection surface.
3 PLANs with matching SUMMARYs. Phase progression discuss->plan->implement->review correct. CONTEXT.json has 11 decisions. No multi-agent violations (serial execution).
Linear directory scans bounded by file count (~16 skills, ~20 commands). No N+1, no unbounded operations.
Clean separation: parser in workflow_utils, validation in workflow_validate_core, review tables delegated to skill files. Good naming, docstrings, type hints.
No formal unit tests for parse_skill_frontmatter(). Verification commands serve as integration tests. Pre-existing pattern: workflow scripts rely on validation commands, not a test suite.
All 32 changed files directly serve the skills-usage-audit feature. No drive-by refactors. Schema rename (karpathyChecklist->operatingPrinciples) was a planned task.
